Title: Solution Architect series: Plan application lifecycle management for Power Platform
Description: Solution architects for Microsoft Power Platform need to define the environment strategy and application lifecycle management (ALM) for transporting work from development to test to production.

Title: Excel automation in Power Automate desktop app
Description: Learn how to use Power Automate desktop app to open, read, and manipulate Excel files with various Microsoft Excel actions.

Title: Configure a semantic model
Description: Semantic models organize complex data into an intuitive structure, enhancing data visualization and enabling efficient, insightful reporting for better decision-making.

Title: Describe the business value of Microsoft Power Platform services
Description: This module focuses on the challenges organizations face in building efficient solutions, automating workflows, and analyzing data—and how the Microsoft Power Platform addresses these needs with its suite of low-code tools. You explored the main parts of the platform, including Power Apps for building custom apps, Power Automate for automating workflows, Power BI for analyzing and visualizing data, and Power Pages for creating websites. You also learned about supporting tools like Dataverse for managing data, Power Fx for writing formulas, and Copilot for AI-powered assistance. PL-900

Description: Explore automation in invoice processing with Teams integration, human approval mechanisms, and Outlook-based notifications.

Distinguish the index-based and real-time knowledge approaches and configure authentication for publishing knowledge-grounded agents.

Title: Describe the capabilities of Microsoft Power Pages

We examine how it integrates seamlessly with Microsoft Dataverse and other Power Platform tools to foster a connected ecosystem for data sharing and decision-making. Additionally, we discuss how Power Pages empowers organizations to build tailored solutions for various use cases. There use cases include customer self-service portals, partner collaboration sites, and employee platforms. PL-900

Title: Create a Microsoft Power Pages site
Description: In this module, we address the challenge of creating and customizing business websites efficiently without requiring advanced technical skills. We explore how Power Pages and its AI-powered Copilot simplify website creation by using natural language prompts to generate pages, forms, layouts, and content. Additionally, we examine tools like the Power Pages design studio for visual and structural customization, and Dataverse integration for data management. We also discuss security features to ensure safe and reliable websites. Users can build functional, professional websites tailored to their specific needs. Users can build functional, professional websites tailored to their specific needs. PL-900
